Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanction Or Contempt, And Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pioneer 74 Lots, Llc For Failure To Obey Order Compelling Pmk For Plaintiff Pioneer 74 Lots, Llc To Attend And Testify At Deposition
Off Calendar Sep-29-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Dennis Yan, Esq. of Yan Law Offices, Tel. 415.867.5797, for defendant and moving party;
Peter L. Kutrubes, Esq., Tel. 925.939.9600 x101, for plaintiff and opposing party, by Court Call.

The tentative ruling was not contested, therefore the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court vacated its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT 818 GREET STREET, LLC's Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanction Or Contempt, And Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pioneer 74 Lots, LLC For Failure To Obey Order Compelling PMK For Plaintiff Pioneer 74 Lots, LLC To Attend And Testify At Deposition is denied. Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order consistent with the court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge Pro Tem: Douglas Robbins; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanction Or Contempt, And Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pioneer 74 Lots, Llc For Failure To Obey Order Compelling Pmk For Plaintiff Pioneer 74 Lots, Llc To Attend And Testify At Deposition
Off Calendar Aug-16-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Demas Yan of the law offices of Demas Yan (415-867-5797) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT 818 GREET STREET, LLC'S Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanction Or Contempt, And Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pioneer 74 Lots, Llc For Failure To Obey Order Compelling Pmk For Plaintiff Pioneer 74 Lots, Llc To Attend And Testify At Deposition is DENIED in part and granted in part. According to the emails attached to Mr. Yan's declaration, plaintiff's attorney asserts that Mr. Eng "cannot be the manager" going forward. It is not clear whether or not Mr. Eng is still a manager, officer, director or employee of the plaintiff. If not, he cannot be compelled to testify as its PMK. Maldonado v. Sup. Ct. 94 CA4th (2002) 1390, 1398. However, plaintiff's PMK, whether Joanne Eng or someone else, is obligated to testify as to all reasonably available information. CCP section 2025.230. The burden is on the PMK to seek out answers to the questions set forth in the deposition notice. This order is without prejudice to defendant bringing a motion to compel further answers and to seek sanctions from Ms. Eng should she fail to testify as to all reasonably available information. Nothing in this order shall be construed to prevent defendant from deposing Martin Eng as an individual. No sanctions. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: Thomas A. Cohen; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
